title: pyrethrum griffithii
intro: Pyrethrum griffithii is a perennial plant native to the Himalayas, belonging to the Asteraceae family, and is also known as Griffith's daisy or Himalayan chrysanthemum. The plant contains pyrethrins, which are toxic to insects but have been used medicinally for their anti-inflammatory, antiseptic, and antiparasitic properties. The medicinal properties of Pyrethrum griffithii have been used to treat various health issues, including fever, rheumatism, and skin conditions, offering relief from pain, inflammation, and infections. Herbal preparations of Pyrethrum griffithii include infusions, tinctures, and ointments, which are often used topically to soothe and heal skin irritations and wounds.
